get.description: List all test runs in the given project.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of test runs to retrieve

parameters.name: testSpecID
parameters.description: Option to filter results by a certain test spec

parameters.name: newerThan
parameters.description: Option to filter results by date

parameters.name: testSpecID
parameters.description: ID of the test spec to update

parameters.name: testSpecID
parameters.description: ID of the test spec to update

parameters.name: testSpecID
parameters.description: ID of the test spec to update

name.description: Optional - New unique name for the test spec

transcripts.description: Optional - Replaces the transcripts in the test spec. See Generate Test Spec page for details on how to supply this parameter.

description.description: Optional - description of the test spec

modelSettings.description:
Optional - List of model settings objects to use as KB settings for test runs.
Transcripts are run once for each of these model specifications. If empty, test run will default to the current project KB LLM settings. A model settings object has the following fields:

- `model`: [str] A valid model identifier.
- `temperature`: [float] The temperature setting for the LLM, must be between 0 and 1.
- `maxTokens`: [int] The maximum amount tokens to use in an LLM response.
- `systemPrompt`: [str] The system prompt setting for the LLM.

startNode.description:
Optional - The node that the test run should start on. Defaults to the usual start node of the agent. This is a JSON object with the following fields:
 
- `diagramID`: The diagramID of the start node
- `nodeID`: The id of the start node

defaultMatchType.description: Optional - Default match type for validating responses. Options are `SimilarityScore` and `TextMatch`. Default is `SimilarityScore`

defaultMatchField.description: Optional - Default match field for validating responses. If `SimilarityScore` is specified, this must be a float between 0 and 1. Default is 0.8. If `TextMatch` is specified, this must be `exact`.

documentIDs.description: Optional - The list of documentIDs associated with this test. If this test is auto generated, transcripts will only be generated from these documents. Defaults to all documents.

persona.description: Optional - Only used for `bot2bot` tests. The persona to base the generated transcript off of.

type.description: Optional - If you would like to specify a `bot2bot` test, do so here. A bot2bot test will generate transcripts based on the given persona.

maxTokens.description: Optional - The maximum amount of tokens you would like to spend on generated transcripts. If this number is too low, no transcripts will be generated.

get.description: Get details of a given test spec.

parameters.name: testSpecID
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the test spec to retrieve.

get.description: List all test specs in the given project.

post.description: Add a new test spec to your project. This endpoint supports automatic transcript generation along with manual transcript upload.

name.description: Unique name for the test spec

transcripts.description:
Optional - Only specify this field if you do not want auto generated transcripts.
This defines the transcripts to run through your assistant when the spec is run. In this param, you should supply a list of `transcripts`.
A `transcript` is a list of `messages`, and a `message` object has the following fields:

`role`: **[str] [required]** Must be one of `user` or `assistant`. This defines who is speaking in the current message. If `user` is specified, a message will be sent in the conversation. If `assistant` is specified, a response will be expected in the conversation.

`content`: **[str] [optional]** Specify this in a `user` message to send to the assistant, or an `assistant` message that you do not wish to validate.

`validationOptions`: **[optional]** Options for validating the assistant response. Only specify this in `assistant` messages that you want to validate. If you don’t want to validate the assistant response and are only including the turn to advance the conversation, use the `content` field described below. validationOptions consist of the following:
- `text`: **[required]** The expected response from the assistant. The actual response will be compared to this field for validation.
- `matchType`: **[str] [optional]** Override for `defaultMatchType` on this particular validation. Must be one of `SimilarityScore` or `TextMatch`
- `matchField`: **[float | str] [optional]** Override for `defaultMatchField` on this particular validation. If `SimilarityScore` is specified, this must be a float between 0 and 1. Default is 0.8. If `TextMatch` is specified, this must be `exact`.
